title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
summary: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4's multi-agent capabilities by running 10 independent subagents on a single local machine, with an orchestrator delegating tasks to subagents that autonomously complete work like SVG art generation. The demonstration achieves inference speeds above 170 tokens per second and showcases batch processing with orchestrator-and-subagent task delegation for enterprise and on-prem deployments.
writeup: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4's multi-agent capabilities by running 10 independent subagents on a single local machine, with an orchestrator delegating tasks to subagents that autonomously complete work such as SVG art generation. The demonstration reaches inference speeds above 170 tokens per second and shows batch processing through orchestrator-and-subagent delegation. The framing targets enterprise and on-prem deployments, where local inference keeps the workload on a single machine rather than depending on external services.
